explorationLog.title: Should we create a single design system or allow college-level variations?
explorationLog.details: Central question for the design theme. A unified design system would ensure consistency but limit colleges' ability to express their unique identity. Need to explore what 'flexibility within consistency' looks like in practice.

explorationLog.title: Tiered template system — university-wide base + college color/image overlays
explorationLog.details: What if we created a core set of templates that every college uses, but with designated zones where they can insert their own photography, accent colors, and unit-specific messaging? This balances brand consistency with local ownership.

explorationLog.title: Who maintains templates after consolidation?
explorationLog.details: If we centralize template creation, who handles updates and maintenance? Is this a dedicated role, or distributed across the team?

meetingNotes.keyPoints: Discussed the tension between brand consistency and college identity. Agreed that a tiered approach could work. Reviewed examples from other universities that have consolidated marketing.
meetingNotes.decisions: Will create a working document comparing 3 template governance models before next meeting.
meetingNotes.questionsRaised: How do other Big East schools handle this? Can we get examples from Miami (OH) or Ball State?
meetingNotes.actionItems:
Rami to research peer institution approaches.
Sarah to draft initial template tier structure.
Jordan to list all current college-specific templates in use.

tasks.task: Research peer institution template governance models

themeNotes:
Initial focus areas:
- Template governance & ownership
- Design system flexibility vs. consistency
- Asset management after consolidation

Key contacts to loop in later: University Web Team, IT Design Services
